codeReview.summary: Implementation covers the feature scope: a polite aria-live dashboard state announcer, navigator.onLine-driven offline banner, reconnect callback on online transition, and guarded reconnect error handling to avoid uncaught promise rejections.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker explicitly deviated from ui-screen-worker step 4 (RED-first tests) for new leaf components; this pattern appears common enough that the skill could better describe an acceptable RED-first flow for brand-new files.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoffs/2026-04-23T01-52-58-409Z__m7-a11y-offline-banner__86afe32a-45f4-40fc-8e4a-07284c6758cb.json:140-149 (followedProcedure=false; deviation on RED-first); .factory/skills/ui-screen-worker/SKILL.md:33 (Write failing tests first).

sharedStateObservations.observation: There is contract-language drift for offline behavior: feature text defines navigator.onLine toggling as the trigger, while VAL-CROSS-026 describes all-relays-disconnected readiness. This can produce inconsistent implementation/validation expectations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: missions/.../features.json:3155-3163 (navigator.onLine-triggered banner expectation) vs missions/.../validation-contract.md:1859-1863 (all configured relays drop to non-OPEN).

summary: Reviewed commit 9d46842 for m7-a11y-offline-banner and found it aligned with the feature's described behavior. No blocking code defects were identified; noted two shared-state observations (skill RED-first guidance and offline trigger wording drift between feature metadata and validation contract).
